era_context: Identical to -2230: Old Kingdom Egypt in terminal decline; Akkadian Empire fragmenting under Gutian pressure; Indus Valley Civilization in mature Harappan phase; Xia Dynasty (if historical) in early-to-middle period in China.

events.title: Akkadian Empire: continued fragmentation and Gutian pressure
events.description: The Akkadian Empire continues its decline. Regional governors and city-states increasingly assert autonomy. Gutian incursions from the northeast persist. This year falls within the broader period of Akkadian collapse (c. 2230–2150 BCE), but no specific events are attested for this particular year in surviving sources.

sources.name: Sumerian King List and cuneiform administrative records

events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for -2231 BCE. This entry reflects the broader historical context of the period. Dating is approximate within a ±10-year window.

events.title: Egypt: Old Kingdom decline continues; nomarchal autonomy increases
events.description: The Sixth Dynasty continues its decline. No specific events are documented for this year, but the broader trend of nomarchal consolidation and central authority erosion continues. Regional governors maintain and expand their power bases.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for -2231 BCE. This entry reflects the broader historical context. Egyptian chronology for this period is uncertain by ±10–20 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. This year is essentially undocumented in surviving sources; the entries above reflect broader era context rather than specific attested events.
historiographic_note: This year is essentially invisible in the historical record. No specific events are documented in surviving sources for -2231 BCE. The entries above are contextual rather than event-based. This illustrates the severe documentation gaps for the third millennium BCE outside the major literate civilizations.
